title: Experiment schematic
question: What happened on each trial and how are trials nested?
axes_and_layout: Left-to-right task stages and dataset counts
marks_and_colors: Pastel boxes are trial phases; arrows indicate order
uncertainty: No uncertainty is shown
takeaway: The analysis preserves run-level temporal order
caveat: This is a redraw from the paper's methods, not original artwork

title: Responses relative to the stimulus
question: How tightly did reports cluster around the true direction?
axes_and_layout: Columns are prior widths; rows are coherence; x is response minus stimulus
marks_and_colors: Blue lines are observed proportions; zero marks the stimulus
uncertainty: Descriptive histograms
takeaway: Higher coherence produces narrower stimulus-centered responses
caveat: Distribution shape alone does not identify latent states

title: Responses relative to the prior
question: How strongly did reports cluster around the learned prior?
axes_and_layout: Columns are prior widths; rows are coherence; x is response minus prior
marks_and_colors: Blue lines are observed proportions; zero marks the prior
uncertainty: Descriptive histograms
takeaway: Narrow priors and weak sensory evidence increase prior-centered responses
caveat: Prior-centered responses need not imply deliberate prior choice

title: Absolute held-out performance
question: Which model best predicts unseen run sequences?
axes_and_layout: Y lists models; x is mean held-out LL/trial
marks_and_colors: Longer bars toward zero are better
uncertainty: Fold standard errors appear in the publication figure
takeaway: Covariate HMM is best at -0.8133 LL/trial
caveat: Absolute LL values depend on response-density units; compare models on the same trials

title: Paired model differences
question: How much does each model improve on independent Switching?
axes_and_layout: X is the paired per-trial log-likelihood difference; zero means no advantage
marks_and_colors: Positive marks favor the comparison model
uncertainty: 95% sequence-bootstrap intervals are in the exact table
takeaway: Static HMM density ratio 1.064; covariate HMM ratio 1.096
caveat: A density ratio is not classification accuracy

title: Static HMM transition matrix
question: Do inferred states persist across adjacent trials?
axes_and_layout: Rows are previous state; columns are next state
marks_and_colors: Darker cells indicate larger transition probabilities
uncertainty: Point estimates from the final all-data fit
takeaway: Sensory self-transition 0.937; prior self-transition 0.964
caveat: States are inferred computational labels, not directly observed mental states

title: Emission concentration
question: Do fitted state labels behave as sensory and prior states should?
axes_and_layout: X lists coherence/prior-width conditions; y is von Mises concentration
marks_and_colors: Blue is sensory; orange is prior
uncertainty: Point estimates from the final static HMM
takeaway: Sensory precision rises with coherence and prior precision falls with prior width
caveat: Concentration is not a probability and should be translated to circular spread

title: Posterior occupancy and representative sequence
question: When are sensory, prior, and lapse states inferred?
axes_and_layout: Condition panels show posterior state probability; sequence panel follows trials
marks_and_colors: State colors encode posterior probabilities
uncertainty: Posterior probabilities condition on the fitted model
takeaway: Occupancy changes with coherence, prior width, and conflict and forms temporal runs
caveat: The representative sequence is selected deterministically, not as the strongest example

title: Subject-level persistence
question: Is persistence only a group-average artifact?
axes_and_layout: X is subject; y is self-transition probability
marks_and_colors: Filled points are self-transitions; open points in the publication figure are stationary baselines
uncertainty: Separate 25-restart fits per subject
takeaway: Mean sensory=0.867; prior=0.839
caveat: Separate fits are empirical-Bayes summaries, not a hierarchical posterior

title: Serial-dependence controls
question: Can attraction to only the previous trial explain the HMM advantage?
axes_and_layout: Model differences and fitted one-back attraction coefficients
marks_and_colors: Intervals crossing zero indicate unstable predictive improvement
uncertainty: Paired sequence-bootstrap intervals
takeaway: One-back baselines are essentially tied with independent Switching
caveat: Small fitted coefficients do not rule out every possible history effect

title: Covariate transition effects
question: Which conditions change state persistence?
axes_and_layout: Y lists covariate/state pairs; x is probability at +1 SD minus -1 SD
marks_and_colors: Right increases staying; left decreases staying
uncertainty: Conditional sequence-bootstrap intervals
takeaway: Largest contrast: prior_precision for L_lapse = 0.309
caveat: Associations condition on fitted responsibilities and are not causal effects

title: PPC condition metrics
question: Do the static and covariate HMMs reproduce observed condition summaries?
axes_and_layout: Rows separate models; columns show metrics; x is the coherence/prior-width cell
marks_and_colors: Observed points are compared with simulated means and percentile intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Covariate-dependent HMM: 19/60 cells covered; Static Hidden Markov Switching Observer: 2/60 cells covered
caveat: Coverage is an absolute calibration diagnostic, not a formal hypothesis-test decision

title: Static-HMM distributions relative to stimulus
question: Does the static HMM reproduce complete response shapes around the stimulus?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Shows where the static HMM captures or misses stimulus-centered shape
caveat: Visual agreement in one cell does not imply condition-wide adequacy

title: Static-HMM distributions relative to prior
question: Does the static HMM reproduce complete response shapes around the prior?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Shows condition-specific static-HMM prior-centered shape calibration
caveat: Histogram agreement is complementary to the five metric coverage tests

title: Covariate-HMM distributions relative to stimulus
question: Does covariate-dependent switching improve stimulus-centered response-shape calibration?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Directly checks the final covariate HMM under the observed design
caveat: Improvement over the static HMM does not by itself establish absolute adequacy

title: Covariate-HMM distributions relative to prior
question: Does the covariate HMM reproduce prior-centered response shapes?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Shows where dynamic transitions do or do not repair prior-centered mismatch
caveat: Transition covariates are conditional predictors, not causal manipulations

title: State run-length calibration
question: Does each HMM reproduce its own inferred temporal persistence after simulation?
axes_and_layout: Panels separate models; x is state; y is mean run length in trials
marks_and_colors: Observed smoothed marginal-MAP runs are compared with simulated latent-state runs
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Each model is calibrated against its own observed posterior state summary
caveat: Marginal-MAP sequences are forward-backward summaries, not Viterbi paths or observed ground truth

title: All-trial versus decoded S/P-only coverage
question: Does removing symmetrically decoded lapse trials improve condition-metric calibration?
axes_and_layout: Panels separate models; x lists metrics; y is the fraction of 12 condition cells covered
marks_and_colors: Blue bars are the complete-model PPC; orange bars are decoded S/P-only sensitivity results
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Covariate-dependent HMM improved: 19/60 to 24/60; Static Hidden Markov Switching Observer deteriorated: 2/60 to 0/60
caveat: The all-trial complete-model PPC remains primary; exclusion uses the complete sequence, is not Viterbi decoding, and cannot be an online lapse detector

title: Decoded S/P-only condition metric
question: How do observed and simulated mean absolute errors compare after symmetric lapse exclusion?
axes_and_layout: X is coherence/prior-width condition; y is mean absolute error
marks_and_colors: Lines separate observed and simulated summaries for each model
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Covariate-dependent HMM improved: 19/60 to 24/60; Static Hidden Markov Switching Observer deteriorated: 2/60 to 0/60
caveat: The all-trial complete-model PPC remains primary; exclusion uses the complete sequence, is not Viterbi decoding, and cannot be an online lapse detector

title: Static-HMM decoded S/P distributions relative to stimulus
question: Does the static HMM reproduce stimulus-centered shapes among decoded S/P trials?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Shows shape calibration after applying the identical decoded-state rule to observed and simulated responses
caveat: The all-trial complete-model PPC remains primary; exclusion uses the complete sequence, is not Viterbi decoding, and cannot be an online lapse detector

title: Static-HMM decoded S/P distributions relative to prior
question: Does the static HMM reproduce prior-centered shapes among decoded S/P trials?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Shows prior-relative calibration conditional on decoded S/P membership
caveat: The all-trial complete-model PPC remains primary; exclusion uses the complete sequence, is not Viterbi decoding, and cannot be an online lapse detector

title: Covariate-HMM decoded S/P distributions relative to stimulus
question: Does the covariate HMM reproduce stimulus-centered shapes among decoded S/P trials?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Simulated decoding recursively rebuilds previous error from simulated responses
caveat: The all-trial complete-model PPC remains primary; exclusion uses the complete sequence, is not Viterbi decoding, and cannot be an online lapse detector

title: Covariate-HMM decoded S/P distributions relative to prior
question: Does the covariate HMM reproduce prior-centered shapes among decoded S/P trials?
axes_and_layout: A 3 by 4 grid crosses coherence and prior width
marks_and_colors: Blue is observed; orange is simulated; bands are simulation intervals
uncertainty: 95% intervals across 100 complete simulations per model
takeaway: Shows prior-relative conditional calibration without changing the fitted model
caveat: The all-trial complete-model PPC remains primary; exclusion uses the complete sequence, is not Viterbi decoding, and cannot be an online lapse detector
